mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate above 7.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening demand for goods and services, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals an increase in supply of new homes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in the CPI above 2.5% signals a monetary easing cycle with inflationary pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with inflationary pressures
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with both declining federal funds rate and rising CPI above 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial jobless claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial jobless claims above 250,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real GDP and a 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a weakening demand for goods and services, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with both declining real GDP and rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million and a 2-month decline in industrial production signals an increase in supply of new homes and a weakening demand for goods and services, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.1 million and INDPRO < 90 over 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both rising housing starts above 1.1 million and declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
